data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4 30b3: Attested signing — pin SHA, record verified_at_sha, in-toto-shaped HMAC, claim-gate coherence); re-confirmation]

Re-audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). This child is the signing/attestation slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child: it CONSUMES the pinned SHA but does not own the re-rooting (its Scope: "Out: ... gate parameter threading (S3)"). Not an absence (S1/S3 cover it).
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER: its AC1 (an attested PASS records verified_at_sha on the ticket and signs over it; re-running against the same SHA reproduces a verdict over identical content), atop the immutable-SHA basis from S1. Heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered SPLIT: THIS child owns the "cannot sign" half (its AC3: a local/dirty run never emits a signature, verify-and-report only); S3 owns the "reads in-place checkout" half (its AC4). Clean enforcer/reader split; no uncovered half.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; no worktrees; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic population, no-worktree) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. (This child's own AC2 — atomic+idempotent verified_at_sha write, overwrite-on-reverify — is a finer-grained pin-consistency guarantee that REINFORCES, not substitutes for, parent AC4.) Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2 (sole owner). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises; its AC5) + S6 (documents; its AC1/AC2). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation; its AC4) + S6 (docs; its AC5). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #4 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot ... never the server's mutable checkout ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; the unsigned local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3. The "signed verdicts are reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable (its AC1, the in-toto Statement envelope AC4); the "unsigned local preserves verify-before-push" clause is reinforced by this child's signing bar (its AC3). No uncovered obligation introduced.

Named-consumer hand-off (covered-by-named-consumer, not an absence): this child's HMAC-trust-model + in-toto Statement docs are explicitly delegated to S6 ("its limits ... are DOCUMENTED in S6"); S6 AC4 claims that obligation. This child's claim-gate ref-coherence AC5 is in this child's own "In" scope. No docs obligation named here is left unclaimed.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on this child — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4 seam — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle, not a G3 union-coverage gap. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, and the ref-coherence clause) are still CLAIMED by a child (this one's AC5), so the union covers them; the WHO-threads-it question lives in the G4 channel.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the signing/attestation slice (record+sign verified_at_sha, atomic/idempotent/overwrite-on-reverify pin, local-mode signing bar, in-toto Statement envelope, claim-gate ref-coherence) of the parent criteria — it is the primary owner of parent AC2 and the "cannot-sign" half of AC3; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
